T, title V, § 501(c)(2)(A)136 Stat. 5389Mar. 27, 2020Dec. 27, 2020Dec. 29, 2022“(a)Tax-favored Withdrawals From Retirement Plans.—“(1).—In generalSection 72(t) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall not apply to any coronavirus-related distribution.“(2)Aggregate dollar limitation.—“(A).—In generalFor purposes of this subsection, the aggregate amount of distributions received by an individual which may be treated as coronavirus-related distributions for any taxable year shall not exceed $100,000.“(B).—Treatment of plan distributionsIf a distribution to an individual would (without regard to subparagraph (A)) be a coronavirus-related distribution, a plan shall not be treated as violating any requirement of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 merely because the plan treats such distribution as a coronavirus-related distribution, unless the aggregate amount of such distributions from all plans maintained by the employer (and any member of any controlled group which includes the employer) to such individual exceeds $100,000.“(C).—Controlled groupFor purposes of subparagraph (B), the term ‘controlled group’ means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.o“(3)Amount distributed may be repaid.—“(A).—In generalAny individual who receives a coronavirus-related distribution may, at any time during the 3-year period beginning on the day after the date on which such distribution was received, make 1 or more contributions in an aggregate amount not to exceed the amount of such distribution to an eligible retirement plan of which such individual is a beneficiary and to which a rollover contribution of such distribution could be made under section 402(c), 403(a)(4), 403(b)(8), 408(d)(3), or 457(e)(16), of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, as the case may be.“(B).—Treatment of repayments of distributions from eligible retirement plans other than irasFor purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, if a contribution is made pursuant to subparagraph (A) with respect to a coronavirus-related distribution from an eligible retirement plan other than an individual retirement plan, then the taxpayer shall, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, be treated as having received the coronavirus-related distribution in an eligible rollover distribution (as defined in section 402(c)(4) of such Code) and as having transferred the amount to the eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.“(C).—Treatment of repayments of distributions from irasFor purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, if a contribution is made pursuant to subparagraph (A) with respect to a coronavirus-related distribution from an individual retirement plan (as defined by section 7701(a)(37) of such Code), then, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, the coronavirus-related distribution shall be treated as a distribution described in section 408(d)(3) of such Code and as having been transferred to the eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.“(4).—DefinitionsFor purposes of this subsection—“(A).—Coronavirus-related distributionExcept as provided in paragraph (2), the term ‘coronavirus-related distribution’ means any distribution from an eligible retirement plan made—“(i)on or after , and before ,January 1, 2020December 31, 2020“(ii)to an individual—“(I)who is diagnosed with the virus SARS–CoV–2 or with coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID–19) by a test approved by the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention,“(II)whose spouse or dependent (as defined in section 152 of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986) is diagnosed with such virus or disease by such a test, or“(III)who experiences adverse financial consequences as a result of being quarantined, being furloughed or laid off or having work hours reduced due to such virus or disease, being unable to work due to lack of child care due to such virus or disease, closing or reducing hours of a business owned or operated by the individual due to such virus or disease, or other factors as determined by the Secretary of the Treasury (or the Secretary’s delegate).“(B).—Employee certificationThe administrator of an eligible retirement plan may rely on an employee’s certification that the employee satisfies the conditions of subparagraph (A)(ii) in determining whether any distribution is a coronavirus-related distribution.“(C).—Eligible retirement planThe term ‘eligible retirement plan’ has the meaning given such term by section 402(c)(8)(B) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986.“(5)Income inclusion spread over 3-year period.—“(A).—In generalIn the case of any coronavirus-related distribution, unless the taxpayer elects not to have this paragraph apply for any taxable year, any amount required to be included in gross income for such taxable year shall be so included ratably over the 3-taxable-year period beginning with such taxable year.“(B).—Special ruleFor purposes of subparagraph (A), rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (E) of section 408A(d)(3) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 shall apply.“(6)Special rules.—“(A).—Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and distributions.“(B).—Coronavirus-related distributions treated as meeting plan distribution requirementsFor purposes of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, a coronavirus-related distribution shall be children.heading: Income inclusion
children.label: 1 Income inclusion
children.text: Except as otherwise provided in this chapter, gross income includes any amount received as an annuity (whether for a period certain or during one or more lives) under an annuity, endowment, or life insurance contract.

children.label: A
children.text: such portion shall be treated as a separate contract for purposes of this section,

children.label: B
children.text: for purposes of applying subsections (b), (c), and (e), the investment in the contract shall be allocated pro rata between each portion of the contract from which amounts are received as an annuity and the portion of the contract from which amounts are not received as an annuity, and

children.label: C
children.text: a separate annuity starting date under subsection (c)(4) shall be determined with respect to each portion of the contract from which amounts are received as an annuity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Gross income does not include that part of any amount received as an annuity under an annuity, endowment, or life insurance contract which bears the same ratio to such amount as the investment in the contract (as of the annuity starting date) bears to the expected return under the contract (as of such date).

children.heading: Exclusion limited to investment
children.label: 2 Exclusion limited to investment
children.text: The portion of any amount received as an annuity which is excluded from gross income under paragraph (1) shall not exceed the unrecovered investment in the contract immediately before the receipt of such amount.

children.heading: Deduction where annuity payments cease before entire investment recovered
children.label: 3 Deduction where annuity payments cease before entire investment recovered
i
children.text: after the annuity starting date, payments as an annuity under the contract cease by reason of the death of an annuitant, and

children.label: ii
children.text: as of the date of such cessation, there is unrecovered investment in the contract,

children.heading: Payments to other persons
children.label: B Payments to other persons
children.text: In the case of any contract which provides for payments meeting the requirements of subparagraphs (B) and (C) of subsection (c)(2), the deduction under subparagraph (A) shall be allowed to the person entitled to such payments for the taxable year in which such payments are received.

children.heading: Net operating loss deductions provided
children.label: C Net operating loss deductions provided
children.text: For purposes of section 172, a deduction allowed under this paragraph shall be treated as if it were attributable to a trade or business of the taxpayer.

children.text: the investment in the contract (determined without regard to subsection (c)(2)) as of the annuity starting date, reduced by

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount received under the contract on or after such annuity starting date and before the date as of which the determination is being made, to the extent such amount was excludable from gross income under this subtitle.

children.label: A
children.text: the aggregate amount of premiums or other consideration paid for the contract, minus

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate amount received under the contract before such date, to the extent that such amount was excludable from gross income under this subtitle or prior income tax laws.

children.heading: Adjustment in investment where there is refund feature
children.label: 2 Adjustment in investment where there is refund feature
A
children.text: the expected return under the contract depends in whole or in part on the life expectancy of one or more individuals;

children.label: B
children.text: the contract provides for payments to be made to a beneficiary (or to the estate of an annuitant) on or after the death of the annuitant or annuitants; and

children.label: C
children.text: such payments are in the nature of a refund of the consideration paid,

children.heading: Life expectancy
children.label: A Life expectancy
children.text: If the expected return under the contract, for the period on and after the annuity starting date, depends in whole or in part on the life expectancy of one or more individuals, the expected return shall be computed with reference to actuarial tables prescribed by the Secretary.

children.heading: Installment payments
children.label: B Installment payments
children.text: If subparagraph (A) does not apply, the expected return is the aggregate of the amounts receivable under the contract as an annuity.

children.heading: Annuity starting date
children.label: 4 Annuity starting date
children.text: For purposes of this section, the annuity starting date in the case of any contract is the first day of the first period for which an amount is received as an annuity under the contract.

children.label: d Special rules for qualified employer retirement plans

children.label: 1 Simplified method of taxing annuity payments
i

children.text: the investment in the contract shall be recovered as provided in this paragraph.

children.label: B Method of recovering investment in contract
I

children.label: II
children.text: the number of anticipated payments determined under the table contained in clause (iii) (or, in the case of a contract to which subsection (c)(3)(B) applies, the number of monthly annuity payments under such contract).

children.heading: Certain rules made applicable
children.label: ii Certain rules made applicable
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of paragraphs (2) and (3) of subsection (b) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

children.heading: Number of anticipated payments
children.label: iii Number of anticipated payments
children.text: If the annuity is payable over the life of a single individual, the number of anticipated payments shall be determined as follows:If the age of the annuitant on theannuity starting date is:The number of anticipated payments is:Not more than 55360More than 55 but not more than 60310More than 60 but not more than 65260More than 65 but not more than 70210More than 70160.

children.heading: Number of anticipated payments where more than one life
children.label: iv Number of anticipated payments where more than one life
children.text: If the annuity is payable over the lives of more than 1 individual, the number of anticipated payments shall be determined as follows:If the combined ages of annuitants are:The number is:Not more than 110410More than 110 but not more than 120360More than 120 but not more than 130310More than 130 but not more than 140260More than 140210.

children.heading: Adjustment for refund feature not applicable
children.label: C Adjustment for refund feature not applicable
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, investment in the contract shall be determined under subsection (c)(1) without regard to subsection (c)(2).

children.heading: Special rule where lump sum paid in connection with commencement of annuity payments
children.label: D Special rule where lump sum paid in connection with commencement of annuity payments
i
children.text: such payment shall be taxable under subsection (e) as if received before the annuity starting date, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the investment in the contract for purposes of this paragraph shall be determined as if such payment had been so received.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: E Exception
children.text: This paragraph shall not apply in any case where the primary annuitant has attained age 75 on the annuity starting date unless there are fewer than 5 years of guaranteed payments under the annuity.

children.heading: Adjustment where annuity payments not on monthly basis
children.label: F Adjustment where annuity payments not on monthly basis
children.text: In any case where the annuity payments are not made on a monthly basis, appropriate adjustments in the application of this paragraph shall be made to take into account the period on the basis of which such payments are made.

children.heading: Qualified employer retirement plan
children.label: G Qualified employer retirement plan
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “qualified employer retirement plan” means any plan or contract described in paragraph (1), (2), or (3) of section 4974(c).

children.heading: Treatment of employee contributions under defined contribution plans
children.label: 2 Treatment of employee contributions under defined contribution plans
children.text: For purposes of this section, employee contributions (and any income allocable thereto) under a defined contribution plan may be treated as a separate contract.

children.heading: Treatment of contributions to a pension-linked emergency savings account
children.label: 3 Treatment of contributions to a pension-linked emergency savings account
children.text: For purposes of this section, contributions to a pension-linked emergency savings account to which section 402A(e) applies (and any income allocable thereto) may be treated as a separate contract.

children.heading: Amounts not received as annuities
children.label: e Amounts not received as annuities
i

children.label: ii
children.text: is not received as an annuity,

children.heading: Dividends
children.label: B Dividends
children.text: For purposes of this section, any amount received which is in the nature of a dividend or similar distribution shall be treated as an amount not received as an annuity.

children.label: A
children.text: if received on or after the annuity starting date, shall be included in gross income, or

children.label: i
children.text: shall be included in gross income to the extent allocable to income on the contract, and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall not be included in gross income to the extent allocable to the investment in the contract.

children.heading: Allocation of amounts to income and investment
children.label: 3 Allocation of amounts to income and investment
i
children.text: the cash value of the contract (determined without regard to any surrender charge) immediately before the amount is received, over

children.label: ii
children.text: the investment in the contract at such time.

children.heading: Allocation to investment
children.label: B Allocation to investment
children.text: Any amount to which this subsection applies shall be treated as allocable to investment in the contract to the extent that such amount is not allocated to income under subparagraph (A).

children.label: 4 Special rules for application of paragraph (2)(B)
i

children.heading: Treatment of policyholder dividends
children.label: B Treatment of policyholder dividends
children.text: Any amount described in paragraph (1)(B) shall not be included in gross income under paragraph (2)(B)(i) to the extent such amount is retained by the insurer as a premium or other consideration paid for the contract.

children.heading: Treatment of transfers without adequate consideration
children.label: C Treatment of transfers without adequate consideration
I
children.text: the cash surrender value of such contract at the time of transfer, over

children.label: II
children.text: the investment in such contract at such time,

children.heading: Exception for certain transfers between spouses or former spouses
children.label: ii Exception for certain transfers between spouses or former spouses
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any transfer to which section 1041(a) (relating to transfers of property between spouses or incident to divorce) applies.

children.heading: Adjustment to investment in contract of transferee
children.label: iii Adjustment to investment in contract of transferee
children.text: If under clause (i) an amount is included in the gross income of the transferor of an annuity contract, the investment in the contract of the transferee in such contract shall be increased by the amount so included.

children.label: 5 Retention of existing rules in certain cases
i

children.heading: Existing contracts
children.label: B Existing contracts
children.text: This paragraph shall apply to contracts entered into before . Any amount allocable to investment in the contract after , shall be treated as from a contract entered into after such date.August 14, 1982August 13, 1982

children.heading: Certain life insurance and endowment contracts
children.label: C Certain life insurance and endowment contracts
children.text: Except as provided in paragraph (10) and except to the extent prescribed by the Secretary by regulations, this paragraph shall apply to any amount not received as an annuity which is received under a life insurance or endowment contract.

children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, in the case of any amount received before the annuity starting date from a trust or contract described in paragraph (5)(D), paragraph (2)(B) shall apply to such amounts.

children.heading: Allocation of amount received
children.label: B Allocation of amount received
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (2)(B), the amount allocated to the investment in the contract shall be the portion of the amount described in subparagraph (A) which bears the same ratio to such amount as the investment in the contract bears to the account balance. The determination under the preceding sentence shall be made as of the time of the distribution or at such other time as the Secretary may prescribe.

children.heading: Treatment of forfeitable rights
children.label: C Treatment of forfeitable rights
children.text: If an employee does not have a nonforfeitable right to any amount under any trust or contract to which subparagraph (A) applies, such amount shall not be treated as part of the account balance.

children.heading: Investment in the contract before 1987
children.label: D Investment in the contract before 1987
children.text: In the case of a plan which on , permitted withdrawal of any employee contributions before separation from service, subparagraph (A) shall apply only to the extent that amounts received before the annuity starting date (when increased by amounts previously received under the contract after ) exceed the investment in the contract as of .May 5, 1986December 31, 1986December 31, 1986

children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this subsection, paragraph (2)(B) shall apply to amounts received under a qualified tuition program (as defined in section 529(b)) or under a Coverdell education savings account (as defined in section 530(b)). The rule of paragraph (8)(B) shall apply for purposes of this paragraph.

if such amounts had been paid directly to the employee at the time they were contributed, they would not have been includible in the gross income of the employee under the law applicable at the time of such contribution.

children.heading: Rules for transferee where transfer was for value
children.label: g Rules for transferee where transfer was for value

children.text: for purposes of this section, only the actual value of such consideration, plus the amount of the premiums and other consideration paid by the transferee after the transfer, shall be taken into account in computing the aggregate amount of the premiums or other consideration paid for the contract;

for purposes of subsection (c)(1)(B), there shall be taken into account only the aggregate amount received under the contract by the transferee before the annuity starting date, to the extent that such amount was excludable from gross income under this subtitle or prior income tax laws; and
the annuity starting date is the first day of the first period for which the transferee received an amount under the contract as an annuity.

children.heading: Option to receive annuity in lieu of lump sum
children.label: h Option to receive annuity in lieu of lump sum

children.text: a contract provides for payment of a lump sum in full discharge of an obligation under the contract, subject to an option to receive an annuity in lieu of such lump sum;
the option is exercised within 60 days after the day on which such lump sum first became payable; and
part or all of such lump sum would (but for this subsection) be includible in gross income by reason of subsection (e)(1),

children.heading: Interest
children.label: j Interest
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this section, if any amount is held under an agreement to pay interest thereon, the interest payments shall be included in gross income.

children.heading: Face-amount certificates
children.label: l Face-amount certificates
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “endowment contract” includes a face-amount certificate, as defined in section 2(a)(15) of the Investment Company Act of 1940 (15 U.S.C., sec. 80a–2), issued after .December 31, 1954

children.heading: Special rules applicable to employee annuities and distributions under employee plans
children.label: m Special rules applicable to employee annuities and distributions under employee plans

children.heading: Computation of consideration paid by the employee
children.label: 2 Computation of consideration paid by the employee
A
children.text: the aggregate amount of premiums or other consideration paid for the contract for purposes of subsection (c)(1)(A) (relating to the investment in the contract), and

children.label: B
children.text: the aggregate premiums or other consideration paid for purposes of subsection (e)(6) (relating to certain amounts not received as an annuity),

children.label: i
children.text: purchased as a part of a plan described in section 403(a), or

children.label: ii
children.text: purchased by a trust described in section 401(a) which is exempt from tax under section 501(a) if the proceeds of such contract are payable directly or indirectly to a participant in such trust or to a beneficiary of such participant.

children.label: B
children.text: Any contribution to a plan described in subparagraph (A)(i) or a trust described in subparagraph (A)(ii) which is allowed as a deduction under section 404, and any income of a trust described in subparagraph (A)(ii), which is determined in accordance with regulations prescribed by the Secretary to have been applied to purchase the life insurance protection under a contract described in subparagraph (A), is includible in the gross income of the participant for the taxable year when so applied.

children.label: C
children.text: In the case of the death of an individual insured under a contract described in subparagraph (A), an amount equal to the cash surrender value of the contract immediately before the death of the insured shall be treated as a payment under such plan or a distribution by such trust, and the excess of the amount payable by reason of the death of the insured over such cash surrender value shall not be includible in gross income under this section and shall be treated as provided in section 101.

children.heading: Penalties applicable to certain amounts received by 5-percent owners
children.label: 5 Penalties applicable to certain amounts received by 5-percent owners
A
children.text: This paragraph applies to amounts which are received from a qualified trust described in section 401(a) or under a plan described in section 403(a) at any time by an individual who is, or has been, a 5-percent owner, or by a successor of such an individual, but only to the extent such amounts are determined, under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, to exceed the benefits provided for such individual under the plan formula.

children.label: B
children.text: If a person receives an amount to which this paragraph applies, his tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which such amount is received shall be increased by an amount equal to 10 percent of the portion of the amount so received which is includible in his gross income for such taxable year.

children.label: C
children.text: For purposes of this paragraph, the term “5-percent owner” means any individual who, at any time during the 5 plan years preceding the plan year ending in the taxable year in which the amount is received, is a 5-percent owner (as defined in section 416(i)(1)(B)).

children.heading: Owner-employee defined
children.label: 6 Owner-employee defined
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “owner-employee” has the meaning assigned to it by section 401(c)(3) and includes an individual for whose benefit an individual retirement account or annuity described in section 408(a) or (b) is maintained. For purposes of the preceding sentence, the term “owner-employee” shall include an employee within the meaning of section 401(c)(1).

children.heading: Meaning of disabled
children.label: 7 Meaning of disabled
children.text: For purposes of this section, an individual shall be considered to be disabled if he is unable to engage in any substantial gainful activity by reason of any medically determinable physical or mental impairment which can be expected to result in death or to be of long-continued and indefinite duration. An individual shall not be considered to be disabled unless he furnishes proof of the existence thereof in such form and manner as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 713(d)(1)98 Stat. 957July 18, 1984
children.label: 9 Repealed. , , ]Pub. L. 98–369, div. A, title VII, § 713(d)(1)98 Stat. 957July 18, 1984

children.heading: Determination of investment in the contract in the case of qualified domestic relations orders
children.label: 10 Determination of investment in the contract in the case of qualified domestic relations orders
children.text: Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary, in the case of a distribution or payment made to an alternate payee who is the spouse or former spouse of the participant pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order (as defined in section 414(p)), the investment in the contract as of the date prescribed in such regulations shall be allocated on a pro rata basis between the present value of such distribution or payment and the present value of all other benefits payable with respect to the participant to which such order relates.

children.heading: Annuities under retired serviceman’s family protection plan or survivor benefit plan
children.label: n Annuities under retired serviceman’s family protection plan or survivor benefit plan
children.text: Subsection (b) shall not apply in the case of amounts received after , as an annuity under chapter 73 of title 10 of the United States Code, but all such amounts shall be excluded from gross income until there has been so excluded (under section 122(b)(1) or this section, including amounts excluded before ) an amount equal to the consideration for the contract (as defined by section 122(b)(2)), plus any amount treated pursuant to section 101(b)(2)(D) (as in effect on the day before the date of the enactment of the Small Business Job Protection Act of 1996) as additional consideration paid by the employee. Thereafter all amounts so received shall be included in gross income.December 31, 1965January 1, 1966

children.heading: Special rules for distributions from qualified plans to which employee made deductible contributions
children.label: o Special rules for distributions from qualified plans to which employee made deductible contributions

children.heading: Treatment of contributions
children.label: 1 Treatment of contributions
children.text: For purposes of this section and sections 402 and 403, notwithstanding section 414(h), any deductible employee contribution made to a qualified employer plan or government plan shall be treated as an amount contributed by the employer which is not includible in the gross income of the employee.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, rules similar to the rules provided by subsection (p) (other than the exception contained in paragraph (2) thereof) shall apply.

children.heading: Purchase of life insurance
children.label: B Purchase of life insurance
children.text: To the extent any amount of accumulated deductible employee contributions of an employee are applied to the purchase of life insurance contracts, such amount shall be treated as distributed to the employee in the year so applied.

children.heading: Special rule for treatment of rollover amounts
children.label: 4 Special rule for treatment of rollover amounts
children.text: For purposes of sections 402(c), 403(a)(4), 403(b)(8), 408(d)(3), and 457(e)(16), the Secretary shall prescribe regulations providing for such allocations of amounts attributable to accumulated deductible employee contributions, and for such other rules, as may be necessary to insure that such accumulated deductible employee contributions do not become eligible for additional tax benefits (or freed from limitations) through the use of rollovers.

children.heading: Deductible employee contributions
children.label: A Deductible employee contributions
children.text: The term “deductible employee contributions” means any qualified voluntary employee contribution (as defined in section 219(e)(2)) made after , in a taxable year beginning after such date and made for a taxable year beginning before , and allowable as a deduction under section 219(a) for such taxable year.December 31, 1981January 1, 1987

children.label: i
children.text: increased by the amount of income and gain allocable to such contributions, and

children.label: ii
children.text: reduced by the sum of the amount of loss and expense allocable to such contributions and the amounts distributed with respect to the employee which are attributable to such contributions (or income or gain allocable to such contributions).

children.heading: Qualified employer plan
children.label: C Qualified employer plan
children.text: The term “qualified employer plan” has the meaning given to such term by subsection (p)(3)(A)(i).

children.heading: Government plan
children.label: D Government plan
children.text: The term “government plan” has the meaning given such term by subsection (p)(3)(B).

children.heading: Ordering rules
children.label: 6 Ordering rules
children.text: Unless the plan specifies otherwise, any distribution from such plan shall not be treated as being made from the accumulated deductible employee contributions, until all other amounts to the credit of the employee have been distributed.

children.heading: Loans
children.label: A Loans
children.text: If during any taxable year a participant or beneficiary receives (directly or indirectly) any amount as a loan from a qualified employer plan, such amount shall be treated as having been received by such individual as a distribution under such plan.

children.heading: Assignments or pledges
children.label: B Assignments or pledges
children.text: If during any taxable year a participant or beneficiary assigns (or agrees to assign) or pledges (or agrees to pledge) any portion of his interest in a qualified employer plan, such portion shall be treated as having been received by such individual as a loan from such plan.

children.label: I
children.text: the highest outstanding balance of loans from the plan during the 1-year period ending on the day before the date on which such loan was made, over

children.label: II
children.text: the outstanding balance of loans from the plan on the date on which such loan was made, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the greater of (I) one-half of the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit of the employee under the plan, or (II) $10,000.

children.heading: Requirement that loan be repayable within 5 years
children.label: B Requirement that loan be repayable within 5 years

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any loan unless such loan, by its terms, is required to be repaid within 5 years.

children.heading: Exception for home loans
children.label: ii Exception for home loans
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any loan used to acquire any dwelling unit which within a reasonable time is to be used (determined at the time the loan is made) as the principal residence of the participant.

children.heading: Requirement of level amortization
children.label: C Requirement of level amortization
children.text: Except as provided in regulations, this paragraph shall not apply to any loan unless substantially level amortization of such loan (with payments not less frequently than quarterly) is required over the term of the loan.

children.heading: Prohibition of loans through credit cards and other similar arrangements
children.label: D Prohibition of loans through credit cards and other similar arrangements
children.text: Subparagraph (A) shall not apply to any loan which is made through the use of any credit card or any other similar arrangement.

children.heading: Related employers and related plans
children.label: E Related employers and related plans
i
children.text: the rules of subsections (b), (c), and (m) of section 414 shall apply, and

children.label: ii
children.text: all plans of an employer (determined after the application of such subsections) shall be treated as 1 plan.

children.heading: Denial of interest deductions in certain cases
children.label: 3 Denial of interest deductions in certain cases

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No deduction otherwise allowable under this chapter shall be allowed under this chapter for any interest paid or accrued on any loan to which paragraph (1) does not apply by reason of paragraph (2) during the period described in subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Period to which subparagraph (A) applies
children.label: B Period to which subparagraph (A) applies
i
children.text: on or after the 1st day on which the individual to whom the loan is made is a key employee (as defined in section 416(i)), or

children.label: ii
children.text: such loan is secured by amounts attributable to elective deferrals described in subparagraph (A) or (C) of section 402(g)(3).

children.label: I
children.text: a plan described in section 401(a) which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501(a),

children.label: II
children.text: an annuity plan described in section 403(a), and

children.label: III
children.text: a plan under which amounts are contributed by an individual’s employer for an annuity contract described in section 403(b).

children.heading: Special rule
children.label: ii Special rule
children.text: The term “qualified employer plan” shall include any plan which was (or was determined to be) a qualified employer plan or a government plan.

children.heading: Government plan
children.label: B Government plan
children.text: The term “government plan” means any plan, whether or not qualified, established and maintained for its employees by the United States, by a State or political subdivision thereof, or by an agency or instrumentality of any of the foregoing.

children.heading: Special rules for loans, etc., from certain contracts
children.label: 5 Special rules for loans, etc., from certain contracts
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, any amount received as a loan under a contract purchased under a qualified employer plan (and any assignment or pledge with respect to such a contract) shall be treated as a loan under such employer plan.

children.label: 6 Increase in limit on loans not treated as distributions
i
children.text: clause (i) of paragraph (2)(A) shall be applied by substituting “$100,000” for “$50,000”, and

children.label: ii
children.text: clause (ii) of such paragraph shall be applied by substituting “the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit of the employee under the plan” for “one-half of the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit of the employee under the plan”.

children.label: i
children.text: if the due date pursuant to subparagraph (B) or (C) of paragraph (2) for any repayment with respect to such loan occurs during the period beginning on the first day of the incident period of such qualified disaster and ending on the date which is 180 days after the last day of such incident period, such due date may be delayed for 1 year,

children.label: iii

children.label: I

children.heading: 10-percent penalty for premature distributions from annuity contracts
children.label: q 10-percent penalty for premature distributions from annuity contracts

children.heading: Imposition of penalty
children.label: 1 Imposition of penalty
children.text: If any taxpayer receives any amount under an annuity contract, the taxpayer’s tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which such amount is received shall be increased by an amount equal to 10 percent of the portion of such amount which is includible in gross income.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to certain distributions
children.label: 2 Subsection not to apply to certain distributions
A
children.text: made on or after the date on which the taxpayer attains age 59½,

children.label: B
children.text: made on or after the death of the holder (or, where the holder is not an individual, the death of the primary annuitant (as defined in subsection (s)(6)(B))),

children.label: C
children.text: attributable to the taxpayer’s becoming disabled within the meaning of subsection (m)(7),

children.label: D
children.text: which is a part of a series of substantially equal periodic payments (not less frequently than annually) made for the life (or life expectancy) of the taxpayer or the joint lives (or joint life expectancies) of such taxpayer and his designated beneficiary,

children.label: E
children.text: from a plan, contract, account, trust, or annuity described in subsection (e)(5)(D),

children.label: F
children.text: allocable to investment in the contract before , orAugust 14, 19822So in original. The word “or” probably should not appear.2

children.label: G
children.text: under a qualified funding asset (within the meaning of section 130(d), but without regard to whether there is a qualified assignment),

children.label: H
children.text: to which subsection (t) applies (without regard to paragraph (2) thereof),

children.label: I
children.text: under an immediate annuity contract (within the meaning of section 72(u)(4)), or

children.label: J
children.text: which is purchased by an employer upon the termination of a plan described in section 401(a) or 403(a) and which is held by the employer until such time as the employee separates from service.

children.label: i
children.text: paragraph (1) does not apply to a distribution by reason of paragraph (2)(D), and

children.label: I
children.text: before the close of the 5-year period beginning on the date of the first payment and after the taxpayer attains age 59½, or

children.label: II
children.text: before the taxpayer attains age 59½,

children.label: i
children.text: payments described in paragraph (2)(D) are being made from an annuity contract,

children.label: ii
children.text: an exchange of all or a portion of such contract for another contract is made under section 1035, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the aggregate distributions from the contracts involved in the exchange continue to satisfy the requirements of paragraph (2)(D) as if the exchange had not taken place,

children.heading: Certain railroad retirement benefits treated as received under employer plans
children.label: r Certain railroad retirement benefits treated as received under employer plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any benefit provided under the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974 (other than a tier 1 railroad retirement benefit) shall be treated for purposes of this title as a benefit provided under an employer plan which meets the requirements of section 401(a).

children.label: i
children.text: the tier 2 portion of the tax imposed by section 3201 (relating to tax on employees) shall be treated as an employee contribution,

children.label: ii
children.text: the tier 2 portion of the tax imposed by section 3211 (relating to tax on employee representatives) shall be treated as an employee contribution, and

children.label: iii
children.text: the tier 2 portion of the tax imposed by section 3221 (relating to tax on employers) shall be treated as an employer contribution.

children.heading: After 1984
children.label: i After 1984
children.text: With respect to compensation paid after 1984, the tier 2 portion shall be the taxes imposed by sections 3201(b), 3211(b), and 3221(b).

children.label: I
children.text: so much of the tax imposed by section 3201 as is determined at the 2 percent rate, and

children.label: II
children.text: so much of the taxes imposed by sections 3211 and 3221 as is determined at the 11.75 percent rate.

children.label: I
children.text: the tax imposed for such period by section 3201, 3211, or 3221, as the case may be (other than any tax imposed with respect to man-hours), over

children.label: II
children.text: the tax which would have been imposed by such section for such period had the rates of the comparable taxes imposed by chapter 21 for such period applied under such section.

children.heading: Contributions not allocable to supplemental annuity or windfall benefits
children.label: C Contributions not allocable to supplemental annuity or windfall benefits
i
children.text: any supplemental annuity paid under section 2(b) of the Railroad Retirement Act of 1974, or

children.label: ii
children.text: any benefit paid under section 3(h), 4(e), or 4(h) of such Act.

children.heading: Tier 1 railroad retirement benefit
children.label: 3 Tier 1 railroad retirement benefit
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the term “tier 1 railroad retirement benefit” has the meaning given such term by section 86(d)(4).

children.heading: Required distributions where holder dies before entire interest is distributed
children.label: s Required distributions where holder dies before entire interest is distributed
A
children.text: if any holder of such contract dies on or after the annuity starting date and before the entire interest in such contract has been distributed, the remaining portion of such interest will be distributed at least as rapidly as under the method of distributions being used as of the date of his death, and

children.label: B
children.text: if any holder of such contract dies before the annuity starting date, the entire interest in such contract will be distributed within 5 years after the death of such holder.

children.heading: Exception for certain amounts payable over life of beneficiary
children.label: 2 Exception for certain amounts payable over life of beneficiary
A
children.text: any portion of the holder’s interest is payable to (or for the benefit of) a designated beneficiary,

children.label: B
children.text: such portion will be distributed (in accordance with regulations) over the life of such designated beneficiary (or over a period not extending beyond the life expectancy of such beneficiary), and

children.label: C
children.text: such distributions begin not later than 1 year after the date of the holder’s death or such later date as the Secretary may by regulations prescribe,

children.heading: Special rule where surviving spouse beneficiary
children.label: 3 Special rule where surviving spouse beneficiary
children.text: If the designated beneficiary referred to in paragraph (2)(A) is the surviving spouse of the holder of the contract, paragraphs (1) and (2) shall be applied by treating such spouse as the holder of such contract.

children.heading: Designated beneficiary
children.label: 4 Designated beneficiary
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “designated beneficiary” means any individual designated a beneficiary by the holder of the contract.

children.label: i
children.text: under a plan described in section 401(a) which includes a trust exempt from tax under section 501, or

children.label: ii
children.text: under a plan described in section 403(a),

children.label: B
children.text: which is described in section 403(b),

children.label: C
children.text: which is an individual retirement annuity or provided under an individual retirement account or annuity, or

children.label: D
children.text: which is a qualified funding asset (as defined in section 130(d), but without regard to whether there is a qualified assignment).

children.heading: Special rule where holder is corporation or other non-individual
children.label: 6 Special rule where holder is corporation or other non-individual

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, if the holder of the contract is not an individual, the primary annuitant shall be treated as the holder of the contract.

children.heading: Primary annuitant
children.label: B Primary annuitant
children.text: For purposes of subparagraph (A), the term “primary annuitant” means the individual, the events in the life of whom are of primary importance in affecting the timing or amount of the payout under the contract.

children.heading: Treatment of changes in primary annuitant where holder of contract is not an individual
children.label: 7 Treatment of changes in primary annuitant where holder of contract is not an individual
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, in the case of a holder of an annuity contract which is not an individual, if there is a change in a primary annuitant (as defined in paragraph (6)(B)), such change shall be treated as the death of the holder.

children.heading: 10-percent additional tax on early distributions from qualified retirement plans
children.label: t 10-percent additional tax on early distributions from qualified retirement plans

children.heading: Imposition of additional tax
children.label: 1 Imposition of additional tax
children.text: If any taxpayer receives any amount from a qualified retirement plan (as defined in section 4974(c)), the taxpayer’s tax under this chapter for the taxable year in which such amount is received shall be increased by an amount equal to 10 percent of the portion of such amount which is includible in gross income.

children.heading: Subsection not to apply to certain distributions
children.label: 2 Subsection not to apply to certain distributions
i
children.text: made on or after the date on which the employee attains age 59½,

children.label: ii
children.text: made to a beneficiary (or to the estate of the employee) on or after the death of the employee,

children.label: iii
children.text: attributable to the employee’s being disabled within the meaning of subsection (m)(7),

children.label: iv
children.text: part of a series of substantially equal periodic payments (not less frequently than annually) made for the life (or life expectancy) of the employee or the joint lives (or joint life expectancies) of such employee and his designated beneficiary,

children.label: v
children.text: made to an employee after separation from service after attainment of age 55,

children.label: vi
children.text: dividends paid with respect to stock of a corporation which are described in section 404(k),

children.label: vii
children.text: made on account of a levy under section 6331 on the qualified retirement plan,

children.label: viii
children.text: payments under a phased retirement annuity under section 8366a(a)(5) or 8412a(a)(5) of title 5, United States Code, or a composite retirement annuity under section 8366a(a)(1) or 8412a(a)(1) of such title, or33So in original. Probably should refer to section 8336a.3

children.label: ix
children.text: attributable to withdrawal of net income attributable to a contribution which is distributed pursuant to section 408(d)(4).

children.heading: Medical expenses
children.label: B Medical expenses
children.text: Distributions made to the employee (other than distributions described in subparagraph (A), (C), or (D)) to the extent such distributions do not exceed the amount allowable as a deduction under section 213 to the employee for amounts paid during the taxable year for medical care (determined without regard to whether the employee itemizes deductions for such taxable year).

children.heading: Payments to alternate payees pursuant to qualified domestic relations orders
children.label: C Payments to alternate payees pursuant to qualified domestic relations orders
children.text: Any distribution to an alternate payee pursuant to a qualified domestic relations order (within the meaning of section 414(p)(1)).

children.heading: Distributions to unemployed individuals for health insurance premiums
children.label: D Distributions to unemployed individuals for health insurance premiums
I
children.text: if such individual has received unemployment compensation for 12 consecutive weeks under any Federal or State unemployment compensation law by reason of such separation,

children.label: II
children.text: if such distributions are made during any taxable year during which such unemployment compensation is paid or the succeeding taxable year, and

children.label: III
children.text: to the extent such distributions do not exceed the amount paid during the taxable year for insurance described in section 213(d)(1)(D) with respect to the individual and the individual’s spouse and dependents (as defined in section 152, determined without regard to subsections (b)(1), (b)(2), and (d)(1)(B) thereof).

children.heading: Distributions after reemployment
children.label: ii Distributions after reemployment
children.text: Clause (i) shall not apply to any distribution made after the individual has been employed for at least 60 days after the separation from employment to which clause (i) applies.

children.heading: Self-employed individuals
children.label: iii Self-employed individuals
children.text: To the extent provided in regulations, a self-employed individual shall be treated as meeting the requirements of clause (i)(I) if, under Federal or State law, the individual would have received unemployment compensation but for the fact the individual was self-employed.

children.heading: Distributions from individual retirement plans for higher education expenses
children.label: E Distributions from individual retirement plans for higher education expenses
children.text: Distributions to an individual from an individual retirement plan to the extent such distributions do not exceed the qualified higher education expenses (as defined in paragraph (7)) of the taxpayer for the taxable year. Distributions shall not be taken into account under the preceding sentence if such distributions are described in subparagraph (A), (C), or (D) or to the extent paragraph (1) does not apply to such distributions by reason of subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Distributions from certain plans for first home purchases
children.label: F Distributions from certain plans for first home purchases
children.text: Distributions to an individual from an individual retirement plan which are qualified first-time homebuyer distributions (as defined in paragraph (8)). Distributions shall not be taken into account under the preceding sentence if such distributions are described in subparagraph (A), (C), (D), or (E) or to the extent paragraph (1) does not apply to such distributions by reason of subparagraph (B).

children.heading: Distributions from retirement plans to individuals called to active duty
children.label: G Distributions from retirement plans to individuals called to active duty

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any qualified reservist distribution.

children.heading: Amount distributed may be repaid
children.label: ii Amount distributed may be repaid
children.text: Any individual who receives a qualified reservist distribution may, at any time during the 2-year period beginning on the day after the end of the active duty period, make one or more contributions to an individual retirement plan of such individual in an aggregate amount not to exceed the amount of such distribution. The dollar limitations otherwise applicable to contributions to individual retirement plans shall not apply to any contribution made pursuant to the preceding sentence. No deduction shall be allowed for any contribution pursuant to this clause.

children.label: iv Application of subparagraph

children.heading: Distributions from retirement plans in case of birth of child or adoption
children.label: H Distributions from retirement plans in case of birth of child or adoption

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any qualified birth or adoption distribution.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: ii Limitation
children.text: The aggregate amount which may be treated as qualified birth or adoption distributions by any individual with respect to any birth or adoption shall not exceed $5,000.

children.heading: Qualified birth or adoption distribution
children.label: iii Qualified birth or adoption distribution

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: The term “qualified birth or adoption distribution” means any distribution from an applicable eligible retirement plan to an individual if made during the 1-year period beginning on the date on which a child of the individual is born or on which the legal adoption by the individual of an eligible adoptee is finalized.

children.heading: Eligible adoptee
children.label: II Eligible adoptee
children.text: The term “eligible adoptee” means any individual (other than a child of the taxpayer’s spouse) who has not attained age 18 or is physically or mentally incapable of self-support.

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: If a distribution to an individual would (without regard to clause (ii)) be a qualified birth or adoption distribution, a plan shall not be treated as failing to meet any requirement of this title merely because the plan treats the distribution as a qualified birth or adoption distribution, unless the aggregate amount of such distributions from all plans maintained by the employer (and any member of any controlled group which includes the employer) to such individual exceeds $5,000.

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: II Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of subclause (I), the term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414.o

children.heading: Amount distributed may be repaid
children.label: v Amount distributed may be repaid

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: Any individual who receives a qualified birth or adoption distribution may, at any time during the 3-year period beginning on the day after the date on which such distribution was received, make one or more contributions in an aggregate amount not to exceed the amount of such distribution to an applicable eligible retirement plan of which such individual is a beneficiary and to which a rollover contribution of such distribution could be made under section 402(c), 403(a)(4), 403(b)(8), 408(d)(3), or 457(e)(16), as the case may be.

children.heading: Limitation on contributions to applicable eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.label: II Limitation on contributions to applicable eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.text: The aggregate amount of contributions made by an individual under subclause (I) to any applicable eligible retirement plan which is not an individual retirement plan shall not exceed the aggregate amount of qualified birth or adoption distributions which are made from such plan to such individual. Subclause (I) shall not apply to contributions to any applicable eligible retirement plan which is not an individual retirement plan unless the individual is eligible to make contributions (other than those described in subclause (I)) to such applicable eligible retirement plan.

children.heading: Treatment of repayments of distributions from applicable eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.label: III Treatment of repayments of distributions from applicable eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.text: If a contribution is made under subclause (I) with respect to a qualified birth or adoption distribution from an applicable eligible retirement plan other than an individual retirement plan, then the taxpayer shall, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, be treated as having received such distribution in an eligible rollover distribution (as defined in section 402(c)(4)) and as having transferred the amount to the applicable eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.

children.heading: Treatment of repayments for distributions from IRAs
children.label: IV Treatment of repayments for distributions from IRAs
children.text: If a contribution is made under subclause (I) with respect to a qualified birth or adoption distribution from an individual retirement plan, then, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, such distribution shall be treated as a distribution described in section 408(d)(3) and as having been transferred to the applicable eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.

children.heading: Applicable eligible retirement plan
children.label: I Applicable eligible retirement plan
children.text: The term “applicable eligible retirement plan” means an eligible retirement plan (as defined in section 402(c)(8)(B)) other than a defined benefit plan.

children.heading: Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.label: II Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.text: For purposes of sections 401(a)(31), 402(f), and 3405, a qualified birth or adoption distribution shall not be treated as an eligible rollover distribution.

children.heading: Taxpayer must include TIN
children.label: III Taxpayer must include TIN
children.text: A distribution shall not be treated as a qualified birth or adoption distribution with respect to any child or eligible adoptee unless the taxpayer includes the name, age, and TIN of such child or eligible adoptee on the taxpayer’s return of tax for the taxable year.

children.heading: Distributions treated as meeting plan distribution requirements
children.label: IV Distributions treated as meeting plan distribution requirements
children.text: Any qualified birth or adoption distribution shall be treated as meeting the requirements of sections 401(k)(2)(B)(i), 403(b)(7)(A)(i), 403(b)(11), and 457(d)(1)(A).

children.heading: Distributions for certain emergency expenses
children.label: I Distributions for certain emergency expenses

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any emergency personal expense distribution.

children.heading: Annual limitation
children.label: ii Annual limitation
children.text: Not more than 1 distribution per calendar year may be treated as an emergency personal expense distribution by any individual.

children.text: the individual’s total nonforfeitable accrued benefit under the plan (the individual’s total interest in the plan in the case of an individual retirement plan), determined as of the date of each such distribution, over

children.heading: Emergency personal expense distribution
children.label: iv Emergency personal expense distribution
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “emergency personal expense distribution” means any distribution from an applicable eligible retirement plan (as defined in subparagraph (H)(vi)(I)) to an individual for purposes of meeting unforeseeable or immediate financial needs relating to necessary personal or family emergency expenses. The administrator of an applicable eligible retirement plan may rely on an employee’s written certification that the employee satisfies the conditions of the preceding sentence in determining whether any distribution is an emergency personal expense distribution. The Secretary may provide by regulations for exceptions to the rule of the preceding sentence in cases where the plan administrator has actual knowledge to the contrary of the employee’s certification, and for procedures for addressing cases of employee misrepresentation.

children.heading: Treatment of plan distributions
children.label: v Treatment of plan distributions
children.text: If a distribution to an individual would (without regard to clause (ii) or (iii)) be an emergency personal expense distribution, a plan shall not be treated as failing to meet any requirement of this title merely because the plan treats the distribution as an emergency personal expense distribution, unless the number or the aggregate amount of such distributions from all plans maintained by the employer (and any member of any controlled group which includes the employer, determined as provided in subparagraph (H)(iv)(II)) to such individual exceeds the limitation determined under clause (ii) or (iii).

children.heading: Amount distributed may be repaid
children.label: vi Amount distributed may be repaid
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (H)(v) shall apply with respect to an individual who receives a distribution to which clause (i) applies.

children.label: II
children.text: the aggregate of the elective deferrals and employee contributions to the plan (the total amounts contributed to the plan in the case of an individual retirement plan) subsequent to such previous distribution is at least equal to the amount of such previous distribution which has not been so repaid.

children.heading: Special rules
children.label: viii Special rules
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subclauses (II) and (IV) of subparagraph (H)(vi) shall apply to any emergency personal expense distribution.

children.heading: Distributions from pension-linked emergency savings account
children.label: J Distributions from pension-linked emergency savings account
children.text: Distributions from a pension-linked emergency savings account pursuant to section 402A(e).

children.heading: Distribution from retirement plan in case of domestic abuse
children.label: K Distribution from retirement plan in case of domestic abuse

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim.

children.label: II
children.text: 50 percent of the present value of the nonforfeitable accrued benefit of the employee under the plan.

children.heading: Eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim
children.label: iii Eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim

children.heading: In general
children.label: I In general
children.text: A distribution shall be treated as an eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim if such distribution is from an applicable eligible retirement plan and is made to an individual during the 1-year period beginning on any date on which the individual is a victim of domestic abuse by a spouse or domestic partner.

children.heading: Domestic abuse
children.label: II Domestic abuse
children.text: The term “domestic abuse” means physical, psychological, sexual, emotional, or economic abuse, including efforts to control, isolate, humiliate, or intimidate the victim, or to undermine the victim’s ability to reason independently, including by means of abuse of the victim’s child or another family member living in the household.

children.heading: Treatment of plan distributions
children.label: iv Treatment of plan distributions
children.text: If a distribution to an individual would (without regard to clause (ii)) be an eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim, a plan shall not be treated as failing to meet any requirement of this title merely because the plan treats the distribution as an eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim, unless the aggregate amount of such distributions from all plans maintained by the employer (and any member of any controlled group which includes the employer, determined as provided in subparagraph (H)(iv)(II)) to such individual exceeds the limitation under clause (ii).

children.heading: Amount distributed may be repaid
children.label: v Amount distributed may be repaid
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (H)(v) shall apply with respect to an individual who receives a distribution to which clause (i) applies.

children.heading: Applicable eligible retirement plan
children.label: I Applicable eligible retirement plan
children.text: The term “applicable eligible retirement plan” means an eligible retirement plan (as defined in section 402(c)(8)(B)) other than a defined benefit plan or a plan to which sections 401(a)(11) and 417 apply.

children.heading: Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.label: II Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.text: For purposes of sections 401(a)(31), 402(f), and 3405, an eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim shall not be treated as an eligible rollover distribution.

children.heading: Distributions treated as meeting plan distribution requirements; self-certification
children.label: III Distributions treated as meeting plan distribution requirements; self-certification
children.text: Any distribution which the employee or participant certifies as being an eligible distribution to a domestic abuse victim shall be treated as meeting the requirements of sections 401(k)(2)(B)(i), 403(b)(7)(A)(i), 403(b)(11), and 457(d)(1)(A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Distributions which are made to the employee who is a terminally ill individual on or after the date on which such employee has been certified by a physician as having a terminal illness.

children.heading: Definition
children.label: ii Definition
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, the term “terminally ill individual” has the same meaning given such term under section 101(g)(4)(A), except that “84 months” shall be substituted for “24 months”.

children.heading: Documentation
children.label: iii Documentation
children.text: For purposes of this subparagraph, an employee shall not be considered to be a terminally ill individual unless such employee furnishes sufficient evidence to the plan administrator in such form and manner as the Secretary may require.

children.heading: Amount distributed may be repaid
children.label: iv Amount distributed may be repaid
children.text: Rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (H)(v) shall apply with respect to an individual who receives a distribution to which clause (i) applies.

children.label: M Distributions from retirement plans in connection with federally declared disasters
children.text: Any qualified disaster recovery distribution.

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Any qualified long-term care distribution to which section 401(a)(39) applies.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: ii Exception
children.text: If, with respect to the plan, the individual covered by the long-term care coverage to which such distribution relates is the spouse of the employee, clause (i) shall apply only if the employee and the employee’s spouse file a joint return.

children.heading: Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.label: iii Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
children.text: For purposes of sections 401(a)(31), 402(f), and 3405, any qualified long-term care distribution described in clause (i) shall not be treated as an eligible rollover distribution.

children.label: 6 Special rules for simple retirement accounts

children.text: The term “qualified first-time homebuyer distribution” means any payment or distribution received by an individual to the extent such payment or distribution is used by the individual before the close of the 120th day after the day on which such payment or distribution is received to pay qualified acquisition costs with respect to a principal residence of a first-time homebuyer who is such individual, the spouse of such individual, or any child, grandchild, or ancestor of such individual or the individual’s spouse.

children.heading: Special rule for rollovers to section 457 plans
children.label: 9 Special rule for rollovers to section 457 plans
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, a distribution from an eligible deferred compensation plan (as defined in section 457(b)) of an eligible employer described in section 457(e)(1)(A) shall be treated as a distribution from a qualified retirement plan described in 4974(c)(1) to the extent that such distribution is attributable to an amount transferred to an eligible deferred compensation plan from a qualified retirement plan (as defined in section 4974(c)).

children.text: In the case of a distribution to a qualified public safety employee from a governmental plan (within the meaning of section 414(d)) or a distribution from a plan described in clause (iii), (iv), or (vi) of section 402(c)(8)(B) to an employee who provides firefighting services, paragraph (2)(A)(v) shall be applied by substituting “age 50 or 25 years of service under the plan, whichever is earlier” for “age 55”.

children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the aggregate amount of distributions received by an individual which may be treated as qualified disaster recovery distributions with respect to any qualified disaster in all taxable years shall not exceed $22,000.

children.heading: Treatment of plan distributions
children.label: ii Treatment of plan distributions
children.text: If a distribution to an individual would (without regard to clause (i)) be a qualified disaster recovery distribution, a plan shall not be treated as violating any requirement of this title merely because the plan treats such distribution as a qualified disaster recovery distribution, unless the aggregate amount of such distributions from all plans maintained by the employer (and any member of any controlled group which includes the employer) to such individual exceeds $22,000 with respect to the same qualified disaster.

children.heading: Controlled group
children.label: iii Controlled group
children.text: For purposes of clause (ii), the term “controlled group” means any group treated as a single employer under subsection (b), (c), (m), or () of section 414.o

children.heading: In general

children.text: Any individual who receives a qualified disaster recovery distribution may, at any time during the 3-year period beginning on the day after the date on which such distribution was received, make one or more contributions in an aggregate amount not to exceed the amount of such distribution to an eligible retirement plan of which such individual is a beneficiary and to which a rollover contribution of such distribution could be made under section 402(c), 403(a)(4), 403(b)(8), 408(d)(3), or 457(e)(16), as the case may be.

children.heading: Treatment of repayments of distributions from eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.label: ii Treatment of repayments of distributions from eligible retirement plans other than IRAs
children.text: For purposes of this title, if a contribution is made pursuant to clause (i) with respect to a qualified disaster recovery distribution from a plan other than an individual retirement plan, then the taxpayer shall, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, be treated as having received the qualified disaster recovery distribution in an eligible rollover distribution (as defined in section 402(c)(4)) and as having transferred the amount to the eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.

children.heading: Treatment of repayments for distributions from IRAs
children.label: iii Treatment of repayments for distributions from IRAs
children.text: For purposes of this title, if a contribution is made pursuant to clause (i) with respect to a qualified disaster recovery distribution from an individual retirement plan, then, to the extent of the amount of the contribution, the qualified disaster recovery distribution shall be treated as a distribution described in section 408(d)(3) and as having been transferred to the eligible retirement plan in a direct trustee to trustee transfer within 60 days of the distribution.

children.text: In the case of any qualified disaster recovery distribution, unless the taxpayer elects not to have this subparagraph apply for any taxable year, any amount required to be included in gross income for such taxable year shall be so included ratably over the 3-taxable year period beginning with such taxable year.

children.text: For purposes of clause (i), rules similar to the rules of subparagraph (E) of section 408A(d)(3) shall apply.

children.heading: Exceptions

children.text: Such term shall not include any area which is a qualified disaster area solely by reason of section 301 of the Taxpayer Certainty and Disaster Tax Relief Act of 2020.

children.label: i Exemption of distributions from trustee to trustee transfer and withholding rules
